topic_name: Iterating Collections & the Collections Utility Class

exercises.title: Trace Iterator removal
hints: Removes even numbers via Iterator.remove()
2 and 4 are removed; 1, 3, 5 remain
Safe: no ConcurrentModificationException

exercises.title: Collections utility methods
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to sort, find min, and find max.
hints: Collections.sort(list) sorts in-place
Collections.min(list) — uses Comparable
Collections.max(list) — uses Comparable

exercises.title: Collections.frequency and shuffle
exercises.instructions:
Create a list with repeating elements. Use Collections.frequency() to count 'java' occurrences. Then shuffle and print size (size stays same regardless of order).

Expected output:
Frequency of 'java': 3
Size after shuffle: 6
hints: Collections.frequency(list, element) counts occurrences using equals()
Collections.shuffle(list) randomizes — size doesn't change
java appears 3 times in the list

exercises.title: Iterator — remove elements meeting condition
exercises.instructions:
Use Iterator to remove all strings shorter than 4 characters from a list. Print removed items and final list.

Input: [hi, hello, go, java, ok, world]

Expected output:
Removed: [hi, go, ok]
Remaining: [hello, java, world]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Use Iterator to remove short strings and track them

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Removed: [hi, go, ok]
Remaining: [hello, java, world]

hints: Iterator<String> it = words.iterator()
it.next() to get current, it.remove() to remove
Build removed list before calling it.remove()
Cannot use removeIf here because we need to track what was removed

exercises.title: Collections.sort with Comparator
exercises.instructions:
Sort a list of String city names by length ascending, then alphabetically for same-length cities.

Cities: Hyderabad, Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Pune

Expected output:
[Pune, Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Sort cities by length, then alphabetically
hints: Collections.sort(list, comparator) — pass a custom comparator
Primary: length ascending
Tie-break: alphabetical
Pune(4) < Delhi(5) < Mumbai(6) < Chennai(7) < Hyderabad(9)

exercises.title: Unmodifiable list — defensive API
exercises.scenario: Return read-only view to protect internal state.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Config` class with internal List<String> settings. `getSettings()` returns an UNMODIFIABLE view. Show that reads work but writes throw an exception.

Expected output:
Settings: [debug=true, theme=dark]
Cannot modify: UnsupportedOperationException
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Config with unmodifiableList protection
hints: return Collections.unmodifiableList(settings)
Caller can read (get, iterate) but not write (add, remove)
Any mutation throws UnsupportedOperationException

exercises.title: entrySet iteration — build report
exercises.instructions:
Given a `Map<String, Integer>` of sales by region, use entrySet() to build a report: print each region's sales and find the highest-selling region.

Expected output (sorted):
East: 8500
North: 12000
South: 6200
West: 9800
Best region: North
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Sales report using entrySet iteration

test_cases.expected_stdout:
East: 8500
North: 12000
South: 6200
West: 9800
Best region: North

hints: Sort regions list for deterministic output
Iterate sorted keys, get value from map
Track max during iteration

exercises.title: ListIterator — in-place transformation
exercises.instructions:
Use ListIterator to transform a list of names: capitalize first letter of each word.

Input: [aisha khan, raj patel, priya sharma]

Expected output:
[Aisha Khan, Raj Patel, Priya Sharma]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// ListIterator to transform names in-place
hints: ListIterator<String> lit = names.listIterator()
lit.next() to get element, lit.set() to replace it
Capitalize each word: split by space, capitalize each part

exercises.title: Collections.disjoint and nCopies
exercises.scenario: Set disjoint check and create repeated lists.
exercises.instructions:
Check if two course lists share no courses (disjoint). Then create a 'wait list' of 5 placeholder slots using nCopies.

Expected output:
Math and CS share courses: false
CS and Arts share courses: true
Wait list: [PENDING, PENDING, PENDING, PENDING, PENDING]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Math and CS share courses: false
CS and Arts share courses: true
Wait list: [PENDING, PENDING, PENDING, PENDING, PENDING]

hints: disjoint(a, b) returns TRUE if no common elements
To print 'share courses': negate disjoint with !
nCopies(n, element) creates immutable list of n copies

exercises.title: Safe batch update with Iterator
exercises.scenario: Complex Iterator removal with state tracking.
exercises.instructions:
Process a list of orders. Use Iterator to:
- Remove CANCELLED orders (track count)
- Remove orders with total < 100 as 'small orders' (track their total for refund)
- Keep all others

Expected output:
Cancelled: 1
Small order refunds: ₹149.0
Remaining orders: 2
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Use Iterator to batch-remove with multiple conditions and side effects

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Cancelled: 1
Small order refunds: ₹149.0
Remaining orders: 2

hints: Iterator lets you accumulate side effects before or after removal
Two conditions: CANCELLED → cancelledCount++; total < 100 → smallRefund +=
Both call it.remove() after their logic

exercises.title: Collections utility combo — top N with stats
exercises.scenario: Multiple Collections methods in one workflow.
exercises.instructions:
Given exam scores as List<Integer>:
- Sort ascending with Collections.sort
- Find min and max
- Count how many scored 90+
- Get top 3 scores
- Reverse the list

Expected output:
Min: 65, Max: 98
Scored 90+: 3
Top 3: [98, 95, 92]
Reversed: [98, 95, 92, 88, 81, 73, 65]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Multiple Collections utility methods on exam scores
hints: Sort first, then min/max use Comparable
Top 3 after sorting ascending: last 3 elements (subList from size-3)
Reverse the top3 copy for descending display
Then reverse the whole list

exercises.title: Collections.sort modifies in-place
hints: Collections.sort(original) mutates original IN PLACE
copy was made before sort — unaffected
frequency(list, 1) counts occurrences of 1 in sorted list → 1

exercises.title: Iterator over Map.entrySet for value update
exercises.scenario: Modify map values during entrySet iteration.
exercises.instructions:
Apply a 10% discount to all products priced above ₹1000 using entrySet iteration.

Input: {Mouse=599, Keyboard=1999, Monitor=18999, USB_Hub=499}

Expected output (sorted):
Keyboard: 1799.1
Monitor: 17099.1
Mouse: 599.0
USB_Hub: 499.0
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Apply discount via entrySet iteration with setValue()
hints: entry.setValue() modifies the map value in-place
Only for values > 1000: *= 0.9 (10% discount)

exercises.title: Leaderboard with Collections — full pipeline
exercises.scenario: Sort, slice top N, reverse, and return read-only view.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `Leaderboard` class:
- Internal `List<Player>` (name, score)
- `addPlayer(Player)`
- `getTopPlayers(int n)` — sorted descending by score, top n, returned as unmodifiable
- `getBottomPlayers(int n)` — lowest n scores
- `printStats()` — min, max, frequency of tied scores

Expected output:
Top 3: [Aisha(9500), Raj(8200), Priya(7800)]
Bottom 2: [Anita(6500), Vikram(7200)]
Min score: 6500, Max: 9500
Occurrences of score 7800: 1
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Leaderboard with full Collections utility usage

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Top 3: [Aisha(9500), Raj(8200), Priya(7800)]
Bottom 2: [Anita(6500), Vikram(7200)]
Min score: 6500, Max: 9500
Occurrences of score 7800: 1

hints: getTopPlayers: copy, sort descending, subList(0, n), wrap in unmodifiableList
getBottomPlayers: copy, sort ascending, subList(0, n)
Collections.max/min use Comparable on Player
Wrap subList result in new ArrayList before unmodifiableList (subList is a view)

exercises.title: Inventory cleanup with full Iterator pipeline
exercises.scenario: Complex Iterator operations on a product list.
exercises.instructions:
Build an `InventoryCleaner` that processes a `List<Product>` (name, category, stock, price) using Iterator:

1. `removeOutOfStock()` — remove products with stock=0, return count removed
2. `expensiveItems(double threshold)` — using Iterator, collect products above threshold, apply 15% discount via ListIterator, return discounted items
3. `consolidate(List<Product> products)` — use Iterator to remove duplicates by name (keep first occurrence)

Expected output:
Removed out-of-stock: 2
Discounted expensive items: [Monitor: ₹16149.15, Keyboard: ₹1699.15]
After consolidation: 3 unique products
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// InventoryCleaner with Iterator-based operations

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Removed out-of-stock: 2
Discounted expensive items: [Monitor: ₹16149.15, Keyboard: ₹1699.15]
After consolidation: 3 unique products

hints: removeOutOfStock: simple Iterator.remove() when stock==0
expensiveItems: collect matching, then ListIterator.set() with discounted price
consolidate: Set<String> seen, seen.add(name) returns false for duplicates → remove
After removeOutOfStock: Keyboard(0) and USB Hub(0) removed → 4 left
After consolidation: Mouse + Monitor duplicates removed → 3 unique
